text: Code reviews are an essential practice in software development. During a code review, a developer’s new code is examined by one or more colleagues before it is added to the main project. This process helps catch bugs early, when they are much easier and cheaper to fix. Beyond finding errors, code reviews promote valuable knowledge sharing. Team members learn from each other’s approaches and become familiar with different parts of the codebase. As a result, the whole team produces higher quality code, and everyone feels greater ownership and confidence in the final product.
